entries.feedback_text:
Hi Loren, thought I'd better explain why I wasn't there.
what a totally awful night, I got myself so worked up I literally could not get to sleep at all , I was wide awake fighting with myself, it got worse and worse like a spiral and i got myself into a right mess, I just couldnt control it , I was so desperate to get to sleep and to make the session and not let youor myself down but the more I fought to sleep the worse it got, the latest time I remember was about 3am , so think i got to sleep at 3am and then the alarm went off at 5am,  I got up and started to get ready but could barley stand up properly,  I lay back on the bed and fought with myself some more , in a mess. I then decided getting on the bike after 2 hours sleep would be for all the wrong reasons at this stage and went back to sleep, thankfully I slept like a baby and have just woken up at 9.30 am
entries.headline: Morning session missed — sleep spiral note to Loren

entries.feedback_text:
Taking a bit of inspiration from the posted set, I set myself a short workout just to get back on the bike and try the TT on the trainer for the first time , similar to the runs, just get on it for a bit .
5mins warm up then 12 mins comfortable 2 mins chilled x 2 . 
gave me 35 mins on the bike, think it was actually enough today anyway, gave my legs a spin , gave everythig a full test, bit uncomfortable and twitch, hard to settle but within expectations. 
I wouldn't say I rescued the day, but I pulled it back a bit .
entries.headline: Turbo - Comfortable / Controlled (27 May) — 12.51 km in 35:00 — avg HR 112 / max 126
